title: Written Investigation Case Studies
why: The closest thing to the real L1 analyst job - and the highest signal per hour of any project on this list.
solves: Completes 3-4 full blue-team CTF investigations and publishes proper reports: timeline, IOCs, ATT&CK mapping, the 5 Ws, and remediation.

title: Enterprise SOC Detection Lab
why: The headline 'looks like experience' piece - a full SOC built from scratch before the first real job.
solves: Builds an Active Directory network, launches attacks with Atomic Red Team, writes detections in Splunk, then investigates and reports them.

title: AI SOC Assistant
why: The interview centrepiece - AI where the SOC actually hurts.
solves: An AI analyst that triages SIEM alerts, explains attack techniques, maps them to MITRE ATT&CK, drafts incident reports, and suggests remediation - built on top of the Splunk lab.

title: Wazuh + Sysmon Home Lab
why: The always-on detection playground where every other project gets tested.
solves: Ships Sysmon endpoint telemetry into Wazuh with custom rules and MITRE ATT&CK mapping, all self-hosted on VirtualBox.

title: Purple-Team Detection Loop
why: The red-team bridge employers love - attack, detect, tune, repeat.
solves: Runs an atomic test, checks whether the SIEM alerts, writes a detection if it misses, then tunes it - documenting the full attack -> detect -> tune cycle with before/after evidence.

title: Azure Sentinel + KQL Cloud SOC
why: Doubles as SC-200 revision and shows cloud-SOC skill with no surprise bill.
solves: Ingests telemetry into Microsoft Sentinel and writes custom analytics rules in KQL, going past the saturated 'failed-RDP world map' into real detection logic.

title: Detection Engineering - Build, Test, Tune
why: The core detection-engineering workflow, executable end-to-end on the current home lab.
solves: Takes a data source through detection logic (Sigma/KQL), validates it with Atomic Red Team, then deploys and monitors it in Wazuh.

title: T-Pot / Cowrie Honeypot - 7 Days of Attackers
why: Real attackers, real data - a week of the internet knocking on the door.
solves: Deploys an internet-facing honeypot on a VPS, logs attacker activity for 7 days, then documents the IOCs and attacker TTPs.

title: Incident Response Case Study
why: A scripted end-to-end IR engagement, written up like the real thing.
solves: Works a ransomware PCAP through Identify -> Analyze -> Contain -> Eradicate -> Recover, delivered as a PDF report using the NIST framework.

title: Phishing Email Analysis Portfolio
why: A literal daily L1 task - and a quick, high-volume win.
solves: Analyses 10-15 real phishing samples end-to-end - headers, SPF/DKIM, URL and attachment detonation, IOCs, verdict - and distils a reusable one-page indicator guide.

title: CTI Threat-Actor Report
why: Cheap, fast, and shows the writing and communication managers prize.
solves: Profiles a threat actor or campaign, maps TTPs in ATT&CK Navigator, derives detection and mitigation recommendations, and writes a BLUF intelligence report.

title: OWASP Top 10 Vulnerability Lab
why: The red-team angle - build it broken, break it, then fix it.
solves: Builds an app with intentional vulnerabilities, exploits each OWASP Top 10 class, then ships vulnerable and patched branches with a writeup and tests per vuln.

title: Intrusion Detection System
why: Understand detection by writing one from scratch.
solves: A Python IDS that inspects live traffic against signature rules and logs alerts to a small web dashboard.

title: File Integrity Monitor
why: A core defensive control every endpoint needs.
solves: Watches critical files, hashes them, and raises an alert the moment one changes unexpectedly.

title: Phishing Page Detector
why: Stops the attack most breaches actually start with.
solves: Scores URLs on lexical and WHOIS features with a lightweight classifier to flag likely phishing pages.

title: Port Scanner
why: The foundational recon tool - built, not just used.
solves: Scans hosts for open ports and grabs service banners, the way Nmap does under the hood.

title: Packet Sniffer
why: See the wire the way Wireshark does.
solves: Captures and dissects live network packets, printing protocol and header detail per frame.

title: Keylogger Detector
why: Learn endpoint defence by hunting the threat directly.
solves: Inspects running processes and OS hooks to surface behaviour consistent with keylogging.

title: Firewall Simulator
why: Understand rule ordering and packet filtering by building the engine.
solves: Applies a configurable ruleset to simulated traffic and shows what gets allowed or dropped and why.

title: Password Cracker
why: Understand why weak hashes fall - so you can defend them.
solves: Runs dictionary and brute-force attacks against common hash types to demonstrate real password weakness.

title: Web App Vuln Scanner
why: The offensive-side complement to the OWASP lab.
solves: Crawls a target site and probes for common SQLi and XSS injection points.

title: Encrypted Chat App
why: Applied cryptography you can actually message on.
solves: A chat app with RSA key exchange and AES message encryption over WebSockets.
